descriptions.solar-energy: Energy captured directly from sunlight via photovoltaic cells
descriptions.renewable-energy: Energy from sources that naturally replenish — sun, wind, water
descriptions.carbon-offset: Reducing or removing CO2 to compensate for emissions elsewhere
descriptions.climate-change: Long-term shifts in global temperatures driven by greenhouse gases
descriptions.greenhouse-gas: Gases that trap heat in the atmosphere (CO2, methane, N2O)
descriptions.recycling: Processing used materials into new products to reduce waste
descriptions.waste-reduction: Strategies to minimize the volume of waste produced
descriptions.circular-economy: An economic model where resources are reused, not discarded
descriptions.composting: Decomposing organic matter into nutrient-rich soil amendment
descriptions.food-systems: The full chain of growing, processing, distributing, and eating food
descriptions.ev-charging: Supplying electric energy to recharge electric vehicle batteries
descriptions.electric-vehicles: Vehicles powered by electric motors rather than combustion engines
descriptions.clean-air: Reducing pollutants that harm human health and ecosystems
